title: Author PROP for Option B (sync manifest) — full structural fix replacing the smart_copy enumeration approach across workspace-sync, CLAUDE.md, build.js
summary: Operator chose Option B over the PROP-017 targeted fix. Reason: the audit surfaced 15 gaps; the targeted approach treats the symptom but not the bug class. Operator wants the structural cleanup that eliminates the 'someone added a write path and forgot to add it to workspace-sync' failure mode entirely. Tinker authors a PROP designing the sync manifest, the migration plan, and the cross-prompt update strategy. PROPOSE ONLY.

context.trigger: Operator decision 2026-05-05 ~17:00Z after reviewing PROP-017's gap audit. PROP-017's recommendation was the targeted fix with Option B deferred. Operator overrides: go directly to Option B as the primary implementation path.
context.pre_existing_proposal: PROP-017 already exists with a sketch of Option B in structural_alternative.options_considered[1]. Use it as the starting point but expand into a full design proposal.
context.why_full_fix_now: Operator-stated rationale: the audit caught 15 gaps where 1 was visible. The 1-vs-15 ratio implies more gaps will appear over time as new agent write paths are introduced. A manifest eliminates the bug class. The 2-3 day implementation window is acceptable; the stale-loop in the meantime is non-critical (just wastes Opus tokens on verified-stale checks, doesn't corrupt data).
context.relationship_to_prop_017: PROP-017's targeted fix is shelved (operator chose not to ship). PROP-017's audit findings (15 confirmed gaps + 11 covered paths + relic catalog + sync_glob recursion note) are CARRIED FORWARD as inputs to the manifest design. Any sync manifest must cover all 15 confirmed-active-writer paths.
context.operator_lean_on_paired_option_c: Option C (integrity-side gap detector) should be folded INTO this PROP rather than filed separately. Once the manifest is the single source of truth, integrity's gap-detector check becomes 'walk the manifest entries, verify FUSE-mtime-newer files all have manifest entries, flag anything missing' — much cleaner than the standalone Option C design. Tinker should design Option C as part of the manifest enforcement story.

structure.schema_design: Define monitor/sync-manifest.json schema. Each entry should include at minimum: path (string or glob), direction (workspace_to_clone, clone_to_workspace, both, never), owner_agent (which agent writes to it — for diagnostic), classification (data | ledger | summary | scratch | source | docs), notes (free-form), added_at (ISO), added_by (operator | <agent>). Include validation rules for path uniqueness, glob format, direction transitions. Show example entries for each classification with at least 3 of the existing 15 confirmed-gap files plus 3 of the existing covered files.
structure.migration_plan: How does workspace-sync.md change? Sketch the new Step 2 that reads the manifest and iterates it instead of the current explicit smart_copy block. Show a side-by-side diff of before/after. How are NEVER_PUSH semantics preserved? (Likely: NEVER_PUSH is a special direction='never' value in the manifest, OR remains a separate filter applied after manifest iteration.) What happens to existing build.js OWNERSHIP and CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules? (Likely: both become derived/duplicated views of the manifest, with a comment pointing back to the manifest as authoritative.)
structure.lint_check: Specify how tinker (Mode 1 or 4) lints that newly-introduced fs.writeFileSync paths in agent prompts are present in the manifest. Pseudo-code OK. Should the lint be in tinker, integrity, or both? My lean: integrity (its job is structural integrity); tinker also has the audit lens but runs less often. Argue.
structure.option_c_integration: Fold Option C (integrity-side gap detector) into the manifest enforcement story. Sketch the integrity check that walks FUSE mtime vs main, classifies each file as in-manifest / not-in-manifest / in-NEVER_PUSH-section / relic-candidate, flags non-trivial omissions. Include severity rubric: a non-trivial agent-written FUSE file with no manifest entry → moderate finding (same severity as schema regression).
structure.relic_strategy: PROP-017 audit identified relic files (FUSE-only one-off forensic artifacts in monitor/analyst/, monitor/decisions/) that a flat glob would force-sync. The manifest should explicitly classify these as direction='never' or 'workspace_only' so they're documented as intentional non-sync without inflating git history. Specify the relic catalog as part of the migration.
structure.migration_steps: Order of execution: 1. Create manifest skeleton; 2. Migrate explicit smart_copy entries; 3. Migrate sync_glob entries; 4. Add the 15 confirmed-gap entries; 5. Add NEVER_PUSH entries with direction='never'; 6. Add relic entries with direction='workspace_only' or similar; 7. Rewrite workspace-sync.md Step 2 to iterate manifest; 8. Update CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules to reference manifest; 9. Update build.js OWNERSHIP if applicable; 10. Add integrity lint check; 11. Run a parity test (manifest-driven sync produces same set of git commits as the old enumeration would, on a synthetic workload).
structure.measurement_plan: How would we know the structural cleanup worked? Define: zero new gaps in 30d post-rollout (caught either by integrity lint or by absence of stale-loop incidents). Manifest passes its own validation script on every workspace-sync run. CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules and build.js OWNERSHIP either eliminated or marked derived. Disprove conditions: any agent-written FUSE file appearing in main without a manifest entry; any manifest entry referencing a non-existent file (orphan).
structure.side_effect_dual_write_rule: PROP-017 noted that the CLAUDE.md Human Notes Rule dual-write requirement becomes optional once workspace-sync round-trips human-notes.json files. Tinker should explicitly address this in the proposal: keep the dual-write rule, drop it, or make it conditional? Argue.
structure.sequencing_within_implementation: Once operator authorizes, what's the implementation order? Probably: (a) author manifest as a sibling proposal artifact, (b) author the new workspace-sync.md Step 2 as a draft, (c) ship both in one commit alongside the integrity lint, (d) verify on next workspace-sync run.

deliverable_requested.do_not_implement: DO NOT create monitor/sync-manifest.json or modify workspace-sync.md, CLAUDE.md, or build.js. Operator reviews PROP and authorizes implementation in a separate step. Treat as one-shot proposal-authorship with implementation deferred.
open_design_questions_for_tinker_to_addr: Should the manifest live at monitor/sync-manifest.json or workspace-manifest.json or somewhere else? Naming has implications for ownership.
Should NEVER_PUSH stay as a separate concept in workspace-sync.md or get folded into the manifest as direction='never'? Argue.
Should the manifest enforce uniqueness on path? What about overlapping globs? Conflict resolution rules.
Should the manifest be in NEVER_PUSH itself (i.e., operator-edited only) or eligible to be edited by agents? My lean: NEVER_PUSH for now (operator owns it); agents propose edits via PROPs. But argue.
What's the rollback story if the manifest-driven workspace-sync introduces a regression? Keep the old explicit enumeration commented out for one rollout cycle?

non_goals: Do NOT propose changes to NEVER_PUSH membership beyond the manifest migration. The audit found no file currently in NEVER_PUSH that should be eligible to push.
Do NOT propose changes to digest-reviews.js — staleness mechanism is correct.
Do NOT propose changes to decider's universal-pusher logic — universal-pusher correctly copies files to FUSE; the gap is on the FUSE→git return leg.
Do NOT broaden scope to other architectural cleanups (e.g., agent prompt restructuring). Focus is on the smart_copy iteration → manifest migration.
Do NOT remove PROP-017 — keep it as an alternative-not-chosen reference.

proposed_fix_spec.note_to_tinker: Operator's lean is the manifest design as outlined in PROP-017's structural_alternative section, with Option C folded in. If you propose a fundamentally different schema or migration approach, argue for it explicitly. The audit findings (15 confirmed gaps, relic catalog, covered-paths inventory) are inputs you can rely on without re-deriving.

completion_criteria.file_present: monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-NNN-sync-manifest.json exists with all 9 deliverable structure sections.
completion_criteria.schema_concrete: Manifest schema specified with example entries (≥3 per classification). Schema validation rules listed.
completion_criteria.migration_steps_ordered: 11-step migration sequence enumerated; each step independently reversible if possible.
completion_criteria.option_c_folded: Integrity lint check designed and integrated into the manifest enforcement story, not as a separate proposal.
completion_criteria.relic_strategy_clear: Per PROP-017's relic catalog, each relic classified explicitly in the proposed manifest (direction='never' or 'workspace_only' or similar).
completion_criteria.side_effects_addressed: Dual-write Human Notes Rule disposition argued. CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules disposition argued. build.js OWNERSHIP disposition argued.
completion_criteria.open_questions_addressed: All 5 open design questions answered with reasoning, not deferred.
completion_criteria.mark_directive_completed: Set status:'completed' with completed_at + completed_by_run + prop_id_authored fields appended per DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit exception.

cross_references: DIRECTIVE-20260505-001 (parent — authorized PROP-017's audit; this directive supersedes the targeted-fix recommendation in favor of the structural alternative)
PROP-017 (gap audit + targeted-fix proposal — operator chose not to ship; carry forward audit findings)
PROP-016 (source-file rescue gap — same bug family, NEVER_PUSH-side)
monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md (the file that needs the rewrite)
CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules section (the duplicated policy that should become derived)
build.js OWNERSHIP table (the third duplication site)
